name: quickjs_wasi
description: QuickJS-NG JavaScript runtime compiled to WebAssembly by the quickjs-wasi npm package, exposing the QuickJS C API with WASI Preview 1 host imports.

tests.description: evaluate JavaScript language features through the QuickJS C API
tests.notes: Creates a QuickJS runtime and context, allocates source strings in Wasm memory, evaluates several global scripts, converts the JSValue results back through JS_ToInt32, and frees the allocated runtime resources.

steps.description: evaluate JSON parse and array access
convert typed array result to i32

bytes.value: let m = new Map([["a", 2], ["b", 5]]); m.get("a") + m.get("b")�

source.description: The quickjs.wasm binary from quickjs-wasi 2.2.0, a Vercel Labs package that compiles QuickJS-NG to WebAssembly with WASI imports and exposes the QuickJS C API.

notes: The npm package does not expose a _start command entrypoint. Instead, the fixture drives the runtime through exported QuickJS C API functions: JS_NewRuntime, JS_NewContext, js_malloc, JS_Eval, JS_ToInt32, JS_FreeValue, JS_FreeContext, and JS_FreeRuntime. The tests intentionally evaluate several JavaScript language/library shapes so consumers exercise parser, bytecode execution, JSON, RegExp, typed array, Map, allocator, i64 JSValue, memory write/read, and WASI import plumbing paths without relying on the package's JavaScript wrapper.
